top_signals.title: Abu Dhabi, Qatar Quietly Slip More Tankers Through Hormuz
top_signals.summary: Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. has been quietly ferrying oil and gas shipments out of the Persian Gulf using its own fleet, apparently clearing both the Iranian navy and US warships to reach energy-starved customers.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: European Gas Drops as US Signals Progress Toward Deal With Iran
top_signals.summary: European natural gas dropped, extending last week’s decline, on optimism that the US and Iran are nearing a deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for global energy supplies.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: China Traders Rush for Exit After Cross-Border Flow Crackdown
top_signals.summary: Chinese investors are rushing to find alternative ways to buy and sell overseas equities after Beijing launched its most forceful crackdown on illicit cross-border stock trading to stem capital outflows.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.
